summary: Hungarian novelist described by Susan Sontag as a 'contemporary master of the apocalypse.' Known for long, labyrinthine sentences and dark, apocalyptic themes. His debut novel Satantango was adapted into a seven-hour film by Bela Tarr. Major works include The Melancholy of Resistance, War and War, Seiobo There Below, The Last Wolf, and The World Goes On. Won the 2015 Man Booker International Prize. Frequently compared to Kafka and Beckett.

works.name: Satantango
works.description: Krasznahorkai's 1985 debut novel. The conman Irimias convinces the inhabitants of a post-communist collective farm to abscond to a nearby crumbling mansion. Allegorizes life under an authoritarian society. Adapted by Bela Tarr (with Agnes Hranitzky) into an extremely slow, seven-hour film. Wikipedia

works.name: The Melancholy of Resistance
works.description: A novel about a traveling circus that comes to a small town with the world's largest dead whale. Mr. Eszter tries to supplant the 'artificial' tuning system used in Bach's Well-Tempered Clavier. Mrs. Eszter schemes to get herself appointed head of the town committee on moral renewal. Allegorizes the rise of fascism. Adapted by Bela Tarr as the film Werckmeister Harmonies. Wikipedia

works.name: War and War
works.description: Georgy Korin's 'life changes forever' after he finds an unknown manuscript about four adventurers and is overwhelmed by its beauty. The epigraph enigmatically states that 'Heaven is sad.' Written in Hungarian. Wikipedia

works.name: Seiobo There Below
works.description: A novel with interconnected chapters on aesthetics numbered according to the Fibonacci sequence. Features a Kyoto heron spearing for fish, a Louvre guard watching the Venus de Milo, and the apprentice Aulista questioning whether his master Perugino has lost interest in painting as he delays the Tezi Altarpiece. The title goddess descends into a noh actor. Features a description of the eyes of a statue of the Amida Buddha. Wikipedia

works.name: The World Goes On
works.description: A 2014 collection of stories. The title subverts Krasznahorkai's reputation as a 'master of the apocalypse.' After '79 paragraphs on blank pages,' the collection ends with the prose poem 'I Don't Need Anything from Here,' which resolves to 'leave everything here.' Wikipedia

works.name: The Last Wolf (and Herman)
works.description: A pair of linked novellas about hunting. In The Last Wolf, a man tells a bartender about his experiences reporting on the killing of the title animal in the Spanish region of Extremadura. Written as a single sentence. In Herman, a game warden decides to hunt humans after a conversion experience. Wikipedia

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Hungarian author born 1954. Susan Sontag described him as a 'contemporary master of the apocalypse.' Won the 2015 Man Booker International Prize. Frequently compared to Kafka and Beckett (by Colm Toibin). Known for extremely long sentences. Countryman of Peter Nadas and Imre Kertesz. Collaborated extensively with filmmaker Bela Tarr and Agnes Hranitzky. Max Neumann illustrated his prose piece Animalinside, about a horrible beast in a suffocatingly small space.

comprehensive_summary: Laszlo Krasznahorkai (born 1954) is a Hungarian novelist whom Susan Sontag famously described as a 'contemporary master of the apocalypse' — a phrase that appears in virtually every question about him. His best-known work is Satantango (1985), his debut novel about the conman Irimias who convinces inhabitants of a post-communist collective farm to flee to a crumbling mansion. It was adapted by Bela Tarr into an extremely slow, seven-hour film, which is one of the most common clues. The Melancholy of Resistance is equally prominent, centered on a traveling circus that arrives in a small town with the world's largest dead whale; Mr. Eszter's attempt to supplant the 'artificial' tuning system of Bach's Well-Tempered Clavier is a distinctive clue, as is Mrs. Eszter's scheming to head the town committee on moral renewal. The novel allegorizes the rise of fascism and was adapted by Tarr as Werckmeister Harmonies. War and War features Georgy Korin, whose life changes after finding an unknown manuscript about four adventurers; its epigraph states that 'Heaven is sad.' Seiobo There Below is a novel with interconnected chapters on aesthetics numbered according to the Fibonacci sequence, featuring a Kyoto heron, a Louvre guard, and the apprentice Aulista questioning whether Perugino has lost interest in painting. The World Goes On (2014) subverts his apocalyptic reputation with its title, ending with the prose poem 'I Don't Need Anything from Here.' The Last Wolf is a single-sentence novella about reporting on the killing of the title animal in Extremadura; its companion Herman features a game warden who decides to hunt humans. Krasznahorkai won the 2015 Man Booker International Prize and is frequently compared to Kafka and Beckett.
recursive_suggestions: Bela Tarr — frequent collaborator, could be asked on his own
Peter Nadas — frequently co-mentioned Hungarian author
Imre Kertesz — frequently co-mentioned Hungarian author
